channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Theatre review: Afterglow explores intimacy but struggles with depth
item.description: The Sydney production of S. Asher Gelman's 2017 play 'Afterglow' attempts to explore polyamory, intimacy, and emotional vulnerability through a physically intense performance. Opening with three naked...

item.title: U.S. and Israel Strikes on Iran Raise Questions About International Law and Stability
item.description: On February 28, 2026, the United States and Israel launched military strikes against Iran, despite ongoing Omani-mediated talks intended to de-escalate tensions. The intervention reflects a recurring ...

item.title: Federal Constitutional Court becomes fully operational after swearing in of additional judges
item.description: Islamabad: The Federal Constitutional Court (FCC) has officially started functioning after two more judges, Justice Rozi Khan Barrech and Justice Arshad Hussain Shah, were sworn in by Chief Justice Am...

item.title: Anthropic Plans Legal Challenge Against DOD Supply-Chain Risk Designation
item.description: Anthropic, the AI company founded by Dario Amodei, has announced plans to challenge the U.S. Department of Defense's (DOD) decision to label it a supply-chain risk. This designation could prevent the ...

item.title: Revealing the 23-F Documents: The Role of the King, Armada, and Civil-Military Plots
item.description: On February 25, 2026, the Spanish government released 153 official files and 89 files from Summary 2/81 regarding the 1981 23-F coup attempt. The documents reveal that King Juan Carlos actively oppose...

item.title: Kristi Noem Leaves Homeland Security as Trump Nominates Markwayne Mullin
item.description: In a significant shift within the U.S. administration, President Donald Trump has dismissed Kristi Noem from her role as Homeland Security Secretary. The move comes amid ongoing debates about the dire...

item.title: Government Plans Fuel-Saving Measures Including Remote Work and Online Classes Amid Regional Crisis
item.description: Islamabad – The federal government has decided to introduce weekly petroleum pricing from March 8, 2026, as part of a broader plan to manage the country’s fuel supply amid rising costs due to the ongo...

item.title: Pretoria petrol attendant uses kindness reward to pay lobola and support family
item.description: Benson Laka, a petrol attendant in Pretoria, recently became the centre of a heartwarming story after helping a distressed motorist, Tembeka Sonkwele. While she was feeling down after losing her job, ...

item.title: Ongoing Tensions and Developments in the Middle East as of March 6, 2026
item.description: The Middle East continues to experience heightened tensions as of March 6, 2026, with ongoing conflicts involving key international players. The situation remains volatile with reported airstrikes by ...

item.title: ANLCA Observes Smooth Rollout Training for National Single Window in Lagos

item.title: Trump seeks influence in Iran leadership amid escalating US-Israel-Iran conflict
item.description: US President Donald Trump has asserted his desire to have a say in appointing Iran’s next supreme leader, rejecting Mojtaba Khamenei, the son of the recently assassinated Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, as a ...

item.title: BJP Considers Five Potential Candidates to Succeed Nitish Kumar as Bihar CM
item.description: Patna: Following Nitish Kumar’s upcoming move to contest Rajya Sabha polls, five BJP leaders are being considered as potential successors for the Chief Minister’s position in Bihar. Samrat Choudhary, ...

item.title: Britney Spears detained in Ventura County for suspected impaired driving
item.description: LOS ANGELES, USA – Pop singer Britney Spears was arrested on Wednesday night, March 5, 2026, in Ventura County, California, after authorities reported her driving erratically at high speed. The Califo...

item.title: O’Sullivan Apologises and Defends Conduct During Ad Hoc Committee Session
item.description: Last week in parliament, private investigator Paul O’Sullivan caused a stir when he abruptly walked out of an ad hoc committee session, prompting the chairperson, Soviet Lekganyane, to consult legal a...

item.title: Over 800 Cyclists Gear Up for Limuru LOOP Safari Gravel Series 2026 Opener
item.description: Kenya is set to host a record-breaking cycling event this weekend as 822 cyclists have registered for the opening leg of the 2026 LOOP Safari Gravel Series at Limuru Country Club on Saturday, March 7....

item.title: Tanzania Banking Sector Faces Growing Consolidation Pressures
item.description: In Tanzania, the banking and financial services sector is undergoing a noticeable consolidation, not because of crisis but due to growth demands, regulatory tightening, and the high cost of digital tr...

item.title: Baikonur’s Area 31 Launch Pad Restored for Manned and Cargo Soyuz Flights
item.description: Russia has restored the only launch pad at Baikonur capable of sending manned Soyuz spacecraft and Progress cargo ships to the International Space Station (ISS). On November 27, 2025, Soyuz MS-28 laun...

item.title: Kamala Harris’s Prospects for a 2028 Presidential Campaign
item.description: Kamala Harris, who lost the popular vote to Donald Trump in 2024 and never won a presidential primary, has not ruled out another bid for the White House in 2028. Since her 2024 campaign, she has large...

item.title: Naija News Roundup: Key Headlines from Nigerian Newspapers on March 6, 2026
item.description: Good morning, Nigeria! This Naija News summary bring the major headlines wey dey trend for Friday, 6th March 2026. President Bola Tinubu don announce say the long wahala over OPL 245 oil block don set...

item.title: Understanding How Overthinking Affects Children and Teens
item.description: Sometimes parents in Tanzania and across Africa see their children procrastinating or struggling with homework and think the pikin is lazy or defiant. But the truth is, many bright and sensitive child...
